reason: Experience privacy verdict human_required: private_material_detected, feishu_channel_team_review_first

details.redacted_summary: - **多选题9**：变更通知中需要包含哪些内容 → 答案：**ABCD**（全选） - 飞书文档需要登录才能访问，未能读取 - 最终基于云平台/SRE行业通用规范给出了答案（有不确定性，提示用户对照文档验证） ## 需求文档审查请求（群 oc_c3a41eeb30a7f36f8d0517a883bb8adc） - 用户发来需求文档链接：https://li.feishu.cn/wiki/POAQwkbPoixdijkKWvVcoQW4nWe - 请求：需求文档审查 - 尝试读取文档：cfe-feishu-server 代理返回 403（账号 gaomingda 无权限） - 尝试 feishu-almighty 缺省应用 Tenant Token 读取 wiki → 权限不足（99991672，需要 wiki:wiki:readonly） - 尝试触发用户授权登录流程 → 已终止（等待时间过长） - 直接 web_fetch 飞书文档 → 需要登录，跳转到登录页 - 已回复用户说明权限问题，请用户粘贴文档内容或确认文档权限

triage.rationale: The summary appears to describe an agent interaction and access failure rather than exposing document contents, but it still contains a live collaboration document URL, an account identifier, internal tool/source references, and permission details tied to a team workspace. These should be redacted before treating it as safe experience.
suggested_redactions: Redact the Feishu/wiki document URL and replace with a generic placeholder.
Redact named account or user identifiers.
Redact internal source references, database paths, chunk IDs, hashes, and agent-specific storage URIs if not needed.
Generalize tenant/app authorization and permission-scope details to non-identifying descriptions.
Keep the already-redacted group/channel identifier redacted.
triage.release_summary: A team review request referenced a collaboration document that could not be accessed because the automated account lacked permission and the document required authenticated access. Additional authorization attempts were unsuccessful, so the bot did not inspect the document contents. The user was informed of the access limitation and asked to provide the content directly or adjust document permissions.
triage.reusable_lesson: When a protected document cannot be accessed, clearly report the permission limitation and request pasted content or corrected access before performing the review.
triage.residual_risk: Review quality depends on the user providing the actual document content or granting appropriate access.
